name: Cova des Bastons / Cova C-11
starting_point: Port of Alcúdia or Coll Baix beach
route_type: Terrestrial, aquatic, and subaquatic (subaquatic section not covered)
approach_time: One hour by boat from Alcúdia port
best_season: Must check sea conditions before visiting
access: Boat or kayak from Alcúdia port. Coll Baix beach is closer but the trail is storm-damaged.
description: A sea-accessible cave with four main areas. The spacious entrance hall (30x54x12 m) features botryoidal speleothems and coraloids. A small 4 m well leads to the Paleonivell chamber with marine phreatic speleothems from ancient Mediterranean sea levels. The Lake chamber is the most beautiful section, with formations and a floating calcite layer covering the water surface. Beyond the lake, 200 m of cave-diving passage contains staff-shaped stalactites.
full_description:
Description.

Entrance Hall

It is a spacious hall of 30 x 54 x 12 m, accessed after navigating the impressive collapse of large blocks that occupy practically the entire entrance. Formations are not too abundant inside, although they can be found in some places. Noteworthy are some botryoidal speleothems in the center of the hall, a small number of coraloids on the east wall, and a beautiful flowstone in the area farthest from the entrance, just to the left of the point where the ascent begins to continue visiting the cave, as well as a couple of stalagmites in the central area and some inactive gours.

The Well

It is a small well located approximately halfway through the cavity that does not exceed 4 m in depth, accessed via a traverse line that was installed at some point by a group of German cave divers, thus sparing us from having to use progression equipment.

Paleonivell Chamber

Measuring 9 x 20 x 9 m, noteworthy in it is a group of beautiful formations that house some phreatic speleothems as a result of an ancient Mediterranean level that today is only visible through said speleothems and the marks that have been left on the walls of the chamber. Calcareous breccias can also be seen in this part of the cave.

Lake Chamber

With its 15.5 x 16.5 x 8 m, it is perhaps the most beautiful place in the aquatic-terrestrial zone of the cavity, as it houses beautiful ceiling formations as well as a layer of calcite in suspension that covers the entire surface of the lake.

From this point, the visit to the rest of the cave can only be done through cave diving for approximately 200 longitudinal meters, in which formations abound, among them beautiful stalactites shaped like staffs.

Note: The speleological outing can be completed by visiting Cova C-10, which is located right above it.
